title: checkPhaseTransition: guard the door to the chain's tail (D1-REVISED)
action: Add `export function checkPhaseTransition(from, to)` to state.mjs, returning {ok:true} or {ok:false, reason}. It is PURE — no cells.mjs import (cells.mjs already imports state.mjs; a back-import closes a cycle). Rules: (a) to==='compounding' is ALWAYS refused from state set — reason must name `bee state scribing-run` as the only producer; (b) to==='compounding-complete' requires from==='compounding' — reason names the required predecessor; (c) everything else ok, including every backward move and to==='idle' (the de-facto abandon verb). Wire it into handleStateSet (bee.mjs:750, AFTER resolveMutationTarget so `from` is read off the record actually being mutated — lanes included) and into handleStateScribingRun (bee.mjs:984), which must refuse unless the current phase is swarming/reviewing/scribing before it stamps last_scribing_run and advances to compounding. startFeature (state.mjs:875) keeps its existing entry guard, unchanged. RED-first tests: legal walk swarming->(scribing-run)->compounding->compounding-complete passes; `state set --phase compounding-complete` from swarming is REFUSED (the exact post-mortem call); from exploring REFUSED; `state set --phase compounding` REFUSED always; scribing-run from planning REFUSED; backward swarming->planning still passes; --phase idle from exploring still passes. Fix the 3 tests currently performing illegal transitions (test_lib.mjs:3418 idle->compounding-complete; test_bee_cli.mjs:524 and :568 scribing-run from planning) by correcting their FIXTURES, never by weakening the rule. Keep templates and .bee/bin byte-identical (parity test enforces).

truths: state set --phase compounding-complete from swarming is refused (the exact post-mortem call)
state set --phase compounding is refused from every phase, naming scribing-run as the way
state scribing-run is refused unless the current phase is swarming, reviewing, or scribing
the legal walk swarming -> scribing-run -> compounding -> compounding-complete passes end to end
backward moves and --phase idle stay legal (hive law 5; abandon verb)
checkPhaseTransition imports nothing from cells.mjs (no import cycle)

trace.outcome: Tail guard lands: checkPhaseTransition + checkScribingRunPhase in state.mjs (pure, cells-free — no import cycle), wired into handleStateSet and handleStateScribingRun. The exact post-mortem call is now refused live. 5 existing tests broke; all 5 were themselves performing illegal transitions and were fixed at the FIXTURE, never by weakening the rule.
trace.verification_evidence:
=== LIVE proof against this session's own state (phase was swarming) ===
$ node .bee/bin/bee.mjs state set --phase compounding-complete
set: phase "compounding-complete" may only be entered from "compounding" (current: "swarming"). That name asserts BOTH scribing and compounding ran; setting it from "swarming" claims work that did not happen and shuts the intake gate on a feature that never closed. FIX: close the chain in order — bee-scribing (`state scribing-run`), then bee-compounding.

=== test suite ===
$ node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs
292 passed, 0 failed
$ node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs
121 passed, 0 failed

New tests added, all passing:
- state set --phase compounding-complete is REFUSED from swarming (the exact post-mortem call); refused close leaves phase untouched (no partial write)
- state set --phase compounding is REFUSED always, naming scribing-run as the way
- state scribing-run is REFUSED from a phase where nothing was executed
- backward move (-> planning) and --phase idle stay legal
- the legal walk swarming -> scribing-run -> compounding runs end to end through the real dispatcher

Full verify suite (all 7): test_lib 0, test_bee_cli 0, onboard 0, portable 0, model_guard 0, write_guard 0, hook_contracts 0
Parity: templates/lib/state.mjs, lib/command-registry.mjs, lib/cells.mjs, bee.mjs all byte-identical to .bee/bin/
